content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

February 13, 1981

Jariath Oley
Deputy General Counsel
Metropolitan Water District of
Southern California
P.O. Box 54153
Los Angeles, CA 90054

A-81-134

Re: Nonsubstantive Alterations to
Conflict of Interest Code of the
Metropolitan Water District of
Southern California

Dear Mr. Oley:

Ted Prim, Executive Director of the Fair Political Practices
Commission, has asked me to respond to your letter dated February 9,
1981, requesting his approval to classify alterations to the Conflict
of Interest Code of the District as nonsubstantive in nature.

As the changes you have proposed meet the standard established
in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18752, they are indeed nonsubstantive
in nature. These changes are therefore effective immediately on
an interim basis.

Sincerely,

Diane Fishburn
Counsel
Legal Division

The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California

Office of General Counsel                                    February 9, 1981




Mr. Ted Prim
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California   95804

Attn:  Diane Fishburn

Dear Mr. Prim:

                    District's Conflict of Interest Code

     This is to confirm my phone conversation of
February 6, 1981 with Diane Fishburn, Legal Counsel,
in which Ms. Fishburn reaffirmed the Commission's position
that the District's designated employees do not have
to file their annual disclosure statements until April 1,
1981.

     The Commission has not scheduled consideration
of the Standard Code amendment to the District's Conflict
of Interest Code until March 2, 1981, which is two days
after the filing date specified in the District's current
Code.  If the Commission's Executive Director is, as
Ms. Fishburn indicated was the case, willing to approve
an interim amendment, pursuant to his authority under
Title 2 Cal.Admin.Code Section 18752, any legal uncertainties
would be substantially reduced, if not eliminated.

     By this letter, therefore, the District is
requesting that a nonsubstantive alteration be made to
its current Conflict of Interest Code to designate April 1
as the required filing date for annual disclosure statements.




1111 Sunset Boulevard, Los Angeles, Calif. / Mailing address: Box 54153, Los Angeles, Calif. 90054 / Telephone: (213) 626-4282

Mr. Ted Prim                    -2-                    February 9, 1981




                         This request, of course, does not constitute
                    a waiver of the District's position on the Commission's
                    authority to amend its Conflict of Interest Code.

                                                   Very truly yours,

                                                   Carl Boronkay
                                                   General Counsel
                                                   [signature]

                                                   Jarlath Oley
                                                   Deputy General Counsel
